questions.question: What did Jesse Pollak note as a known edge case for wallet balance pricing issues on March 13, 2026?
options: Tokens with super low liquidity USDC pair
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak stated that tokens with super low liquidity USDC pairs were a known edge case where pricing was not yet correct.

questions.question: Which company co-authored the Machine Payments Protocol (MPP) with Dan Romero's team?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero mentioned that MPP was co-authored with Stripe.

questions.question: In March 2026, Jesse Pollak celebrated a Cricket World Cup win for which team?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak's post on March 9, 2026, includes the India flag and mentions celebrating the win.

questions.question: What terminal application did Brian Armstrong return to after using Warp?
questions.explanation: On March 7, 2026, Brian Armstrong posted that it felt good to be back to iTerm2 from Warp.

questions.question: What is the name of the mainnet launched by Dan Romero in March 2026?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero announced that 'Tempo mainnet is live!' on March 18, 2026.

questions.question: Which hardware device did Brian Armstrong call a 'piece of trash' that is now 'coming in clutch'?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong mentioned his 'openclaw in my pocket' which he bought 2 years ago.

questions.question: How many payment methods did the Machine Payments Protocol (MPP) launch with on Day 1?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero listed 4 methods: Tempo, Stripe, Visa, and Lightspark.

questions.question: What did Jesse Pollak compare to 'Tom Riddle’s diary' in a January 2026 post?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak stated that LLMs/agents have matured to a point where they can role-play with a knowledge base, making the diary no longer fiction.

questions.question: What specific percentage reduction in global p95 latency did Jesse Pollak report for mcilroyc?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak posted that latency was reduced by 50% for mcilroyc.

questions.question: According to Dan Romero, what is the core design philosophy of MPP regarding the payment rail?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero emphasized that MPP is payment rail agnostic and works with crypto, fiat, or any chain.

questions.question: What was the reason for Jesse Pollak's warning about Farcaster client degradation on February 5, 2026?
options: Significant changes at the database layer
questions.explanation: Jesse mentioned making significant changes at the database layer on Farcaster's backend.

questions.question: Which GitHub repository did Brian Armstrong share as his 'daily find' on March 6, 2026?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong shared a link to github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode.

questions.question: What did Jesse Pollak say his kids are now asking for their own farms in?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak posted that his kids are asking for their own /fruitling-valley farms.

questions.question: In his post about 'unproductive productive time', what was Brian Armstrong doing for five hours?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong posted that his ADHD told him to disconnect and reorganize all cables for five hours.

questions.question: What specific time was the shard 0 cutover scheduled for on February 24, 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak expected instability at 12:00 PM UTC on Feb 24 for the shard 0 cutover.

questions.question: Which entity did Jesse Pollak praise for sending bugs 'right to jail' using linear tickets and magic?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak noted that @grin is on a mission to send bugs to jail as 'the Neynar way'.

questions.question: What does Dan Romero claim about the cost of using MPP?
options: There is no inherent cost to using MPP
It is free for Stripe users only
questions.explanation: Dan Romero debunked the myth that it costs 1.5%, stating there is no inherent cost to using MPP itself.

questions.question: What did Brian Armstrong ask the Neynar team for on March 25, 2026?
options: An API to check if a user is a builder
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong asked @neynar if they have an api to check if a user is a builder (miniapp dev, project owner, etc).

questions.question: According to Base official on March 15, 2024, what happened to their revenue?
questions.explanation: The official Base account posted: 'last week we doubled our revenue and I have no idea why'.

questions.question: What was Brian Armstrong's experience with 'GSD-2' after three days?
options: Love the approach, but just not there yet
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong stated: 'love the approach, but just not there yet' and went back to vanilla Claude code.

questions.question: What is the URL for the interactive demo of the Machine Payments Protocol?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero invited users to 'check out the interactive demo on mpp.dev'.

questions.question: What did Jesse Pollak suggest FAFOing (Finding Out) by giving an OpenClaw agent access to?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak asked if anyone had given an OpenClaw agent access to a humanoid like the G1 to move around their home.

questions.question: What did Jesse Pollak state was 'the Neynar way' regarding bug reports?
options: Linear ticket -> magic -> test + review -> ship -> reply
Fix it in the next shard cutover
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak outlined Step 1-5 starting with a linear ticket and ending with a reply to the reporter.

questions.question: What specific AI limit did Brian Armstrong use up in one day?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong posted on March 8, 2026, that he used up all of 'claude max 20x weekly limit in one day'.

questions.question: Dan Romero's 'New personal ATH' on a flight involved a game that uses which of the following cards?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero's post mentions 'steel cards' and a 'photograph combo', referencing the game Balatro.

questions.question: Which project did Brian Armstrong share on March 14, 2026, calling it a 'cool project' with a video?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong shared nikopueringer/CorridorKey on GitHub.

questions.question: Jesse Pollak mentioned that Neynar Studio's dev container goal is to get to what status?
questions.explanation: Jesse's update for neynar.com/studio mentioned the goal is to get the dev container to 'always ready'.

questions.question: Who does Brian Armstrong say 'thanks for the beep' to on March 20, 2026?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong posted 'thanks for the beep @bigeon'.

questions.question: In the official Base account's 2022 post, what were they comparing early crypto journalism to?
questions.explanation: The post linked to an article 'comparing early internet journalism to crypto journalism'.

questions.question: What does Brian Armstrong say 'sometimes gives you wings' and 'sometimes fucks you up'?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong described ADHD as a 'powerful drug' that can give wings or fuck you up.

questions.question: What is the price Brian Armstrong mentions for an 'ask me anything'?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong wrote 'ask me anything for $0.25' on March 20, 2026.

questions.question: Jesse Pollak's post on Jan 30, 2026 mentions 50% less global p95 latency for who?
questions.explanation: The post specifically tags and links to @mcilroyc.

questions.question: In his March 31, 2026 post, what does Dan Romero describe as 'incredible slop'?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero's post simply says 'incredible slop' with a link to markgadala's post.

questions.question: Which payment rail extensions were mentioned as being able to be added to MPP in 'a matter of days'?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero stated: 'Visa and Lightspark were each able to extend MPP in a matter of days.'

questions.question: What did Jesse Pollak say 'skill issue' has now?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak posted: 'skill issue has a whole new meaning now' on Feb 27, 2026.

questions.question: In Brian Armstrong's ASCII art on April 2, 2024, what is the skeleton holding?
questions.explanation: The ASCII art shows a skeleton holding a pager (📟).

questions.explanation: Mircea mentions 'I built an (incomplete) Python port... link to the repo: github.com/mirceapasoi/hub_py'.

questions.question: According to Jesse Pollak, why were wallet and total balances showing up inaccurately on March 13?
options: Underlying changes to where we fetch wallet balances from
questions.explanation: Jesse stated: 'Working through some underlying changes to where we fetch wallet balances from.'

questions.question: What is the URL shared by Dan Romero on March 11, 2026, with the comment 'Neat'?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero shared wondering.app on March 11, 2026.

questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong starts his post with 'hey docgpt'.

options: It is going to be even more interesting
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak mentions this after talking about coding agents shortening the reward cycle.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a myth about MPP addressed by Dan Romero?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero's post addresses the first, second, and fourth options, but doesn't list the third one as a specific 'myth' in those exact words.

questions.explanation: The update noted: 'securely configure Environment Variables & secrets'.

options: First cast - can’t wait to see where this road takes me!
questions.explanation: The post dated 2022-10-24 contains this exact text.

questions.question: Brian Armstrong mentioned a project that uses 'agent teams + /loop'. What was he using?
questions.explanation: He mentions going back to 'vanilla claude code with agent teams + /loop'.

questions.question: What did Jesse Pollak mention about Iceland and cricket?
options: They have S tier shit posting

questions.question: What is the stated reason for MPP supporting sessions (payment channels)?
options: Many small payments at the speed of APIs
questions.explanation: Dan Romero said sessions are useful for many small payments in succession at the speed of APIs, not blockchains.

options: i'm only gonna say wowow when animated pfps come back
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong posted that he would only say wowow when animated PFPs return.

questions.explanation: The steps listed were linear ticket, magic, test+review, ship, and reply back.
